name: Multiple Vector Attack Source
notes: This rule triggers when a source host tries multiple attack vectors. This could indicate the source host is specifically targeting an asset.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Countries/Regions with no Remote Access
conditions_text: when a subset of at least 4 of these BB:BehaviorDefinition: Compromise Activities, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication to Disabled Account, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication to Expired Account, BB:CategoryDefinition: Countries/Regions with no Remote Access, BB:CategoryDefinition: DDoS Attack Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Denies, BB:CategoryDefinition: Key Loggers, BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Policy Violation, BB:CategoryDefinition: Network DoS Attack, BB:CategoryDefinition: Service DoS, BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Virus Detected, BB:CategoryDefinition: Worm Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Database Access Denied, BB:Database: System Action Deny, BB:NetworkDefinition: Darknet Addresses, BB:NetworkDefinition: Honeypot like Addresses, BB:NetworkDefinition: Undefined IP Space, BB:NetworkDefinition: Watch List Addresses, BB:PortDefinition: Common Worm Ports, in any order, from the same source IP to any destination IP, over 1 days
thresholds: when a subset of at least 4 of these BB:BehaviorDefinition: Compromise Activities, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication to Disabled Account, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication to Expired Account, BB:CategoryDefinition: Countries/Regions with no Remote Access, BB:CategoryDefinition: DDoS Attack Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Denies, BB:CategoryDefinition: Key Loggers, BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Policy Violation, BB:CategoryDefinition: Network DoS Attack, BB:CategoryDefinition: Service DoS, BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Virus Detected, BB:CategoryDefinition: Worm Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Database Access Denied, BB:Database: System Action Deny, BB:NetworkDefinition: Darknet Addresses, BB:NetworkDefinition: Honeypot like Addresses, BB:NetworkDefinition: Undefined IP Space, BB:NetworkDefinition: Watch List Addresses, BB:PortDefinition: Common Worm Ports, in any order, from the same source IP to any destination IP, over 1 days
TD_list: APPLY Multiple Vector Attack Source on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when a subset of at least 4 of these BB:BehaviorDefinition: Compromise Activities, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication to Disabled Account, BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication to Expired Account, BB:CategoryDefinition: Countries/Regions with no Remote Access, BB:CategoryDefinition: DDoS Attack Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Exploits Backdoors and Trojans, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Denies, BB:CategoryDefinition: Key Loggers, BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Policy Violation, BB:CategoryDefinition: Network DoS Attack, BB:CategoryDefinition: Service DoS, BB:CategoryDefinition: Recon Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Virus Detected, BB:CategoryDefinition: Worm Events, BB:CategoryDefinition: Database Access Denied, BB:Database: System Action Deny, BB:NetworkDefinition: Darknet Addresses, BB:NetworkDefinition: Honeypot like Addresses, BB:NetworkDefinition: Undefined IP Space, BB:NetworkDefinition: Watch List Addresses, BB:PortDefinition: Common Worm Ports, in any order, from the same source IP  to  any destination IP, over 1 days
RED_list: Event name: Multiple Vector Attacker Detected
Event description: Detects when a source host tries multiple attack vectors. This could indicate the source host is specifically targeting an asset.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
